name: Olena Kovalenko
title: Senior Frontend Engineer
summary: Senior frontend engineer with 8 years building product UIs in React and TypeScript. I care about performance, accessibility, and shipping. Comfortable owning features end to end, mentoring, and working directly with product and design.

bullets: Led the rebuild of the customer dashboard in Next.js and TypeScript, cutting time-to-interactive by 41% and lifting weekly active users by 18%.
Built and own a shared design system (40+ components in Storybook) used by 5 product teams, reducing UI build time for new features by roughly a third.
Introduced Playwright end-to-end tests and a CI gate in GitHub Actions, dropping production UI regressions by ~60%.
Mentored 3 mid-level engineers through code review and pairing; two were promoted within a year.
Drove accessibility to WCAG 2.1 AA across the core funnel, fixing 120+ issues found in audits.
Built the checkout and product pages for an e-commerce platform serving 2M monthly users.
Cut bundle size by 35% through code-splitting and dependency pruning, improving Lighthouse performance from 58 to 92.
Ran A/B tests on the cart flow that increased conversion by 7%.
Migrated a legacy jQuery storefront to React incrementally with zero downtime.
Delivered marketing sites and small web apps for 15+ clients.
Built reusable Vue components and a small internal CSS framework.

projects.name: a11y-checklist
projects.description: Open-source browser extension that flags common accessibility issues on any page. 1.2k GitHub stars.

projects.name: perfbench
projects.description: CLI that runs Lighthouse against a list of routes and posts a diff to pull requests.
